name: K-Pop
description: BTS, Blackpink, Big Bang, and the global Korean Wave — from Seo Taiji to Coachella headliners.

questions.q: What does 'K-pop' stand for?
Which country did K-pop originate in?
Which 1992 boy band's debut is considered the start of modern K-pop 'idol' culture?

source.notes: Their performance on a televised talent show in 1992 transformed Korean pop music.

questions.q: Which 2018 BTS album was the first K-pop album to top the U.S. Billboard 200 chart?

source.notes: BTS reached #1 on the Billboard 200 in May 2018.

questions.q: Which K-pop group became the first to headline Coachella in 2023?
Which Psy song became the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views (in 2012)?

source.notes: Hit 1 billion in December 2012; YouTube's view counter had to be expanded to handle higher numbers.

questions.q: Who founded SM Entertainment in 1995, helping create the modern K-pop industry?

source.notes: SM ('Star Museum') Entertainment debuted H.O.T. in 1996, BoA, TVXQ, Super Junior, Girls' Generation, EXO, NCT, and aespa.

questions.q: What Korean term means the 'youngest member' of a K-pop group?
How many members are in BTS?

source.notes: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V, Jungkook.

questions.q: How many members are in Blackpink?
What does 'BTS' stand for in Korean (Bangtan Sonyeondan)?

source.notes: Bangtan Sonyeondan = 'Bulletproof Boy Scouts'. The fandom is called ARMY.

questions.q: Which Big Bang single, released in 2012, became one of K-pop's most globally recognized hits?

source.notes: Often associated with the global K-pop boom.

questions.q: What is the term for a K-pop group's release of new music with promotional activities?

source.notes: Even brand-new releases (not breaks) are called comebacks in K-pop terminology.

questions.q: What is the Korean word for the major 'Grand Prize' at K-pop music awards?

source.notes: Daesang = 'Grand Prize'; Bonsang = the supporting awards beneath it.

questions.q: What is the cultural phenomenon of Korean entertainment exporting globally called?
Which Blackpink hit features the lyrics 'Pretty savage' and is from their 2018 EP?
Which 2003 K-pop boy band became the first foreign group to top Japan's Oricon chart?
Which BTS member became the first K-pop solo artist to headline a U.S. festival when he played Lollapalooza 2022?

source.notes: J-Hope's 'Jack in the Box' headline set in Chicago, July 2022.

questions.q: What is the major K-pop awards ceremony, broadcast across Asia since 1999?
What is the term used for fans who are intensely devoted to a K-pop idol or group?

source.notes: Term originated from the Eminem song 'Stan' but became near-universal in K-pop fandom.
